svg.text: 16.11 · Failure & DR — RPO≈0, fast RTO, and what zero-data-loss COSTS
survive node/region loss + catastrophe with NO lost money · the honest accounting of the cost
+ SEMI-SYNC (durable on a replica → survives primary loss, M10)
+ tested PITR for logical disasters (replicate → failover useless, M15/15.8)
automated FENCED failover (promote a replica in seconds, M10)
+ FENCE the old primary (no split-brain, M15/15.3)
+ fast physical/snapshot restore (M13/13.2) for what failover can't fix
+ the full M15 prevention checklist (15.16) + reconciliation (16.7) to verify
⚖ WHAT ZERO-DATA-LOSS REALLY COSTS (the honest accounting)
LATENCY — semi-sync adds a round-trip per commit (M10/10.4) · INFRASTRUCTURE — replicas + cross-region + standby
OPERATIONAL RIGOR — tested restore drills, monitoring, fencing automation · true zero-data-loss is NOT free
the architect's job: QUANTIFY it (latency/cost) + JUSTIFY it (vs the cost of LOSING money — which for payments vastly exceeds it; often regulated)
💰 a committed transfer survives crash (M09) + node loss (semi-sync) + region loss (cross-region) + logical disaster (PITR)
— and is RECONCILED correct after recovery (16.7) · money is never lost EVEN WHEN THE SYSTEM FAILS (the ultimate guarantee)
the synthesis of M15's "catastrophes are survivable" · for lower-value systems, accept looser RPO/RTO (cheaper) — match cost to the cost of loss
